docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Every contributor deserves instant feedback. Every maintainer deserves their time back.

View on GitHub Live Demo Try It Now

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
Open source projects grow when contributors feel supported. But as a project grows, so does the documentation review burden. Maintainers end up spending hours each week going through pull requests, correcting the same jargon, fixing the same writing patterns, and leaving feedback they wrote a dozen times before.

Where projects stall

The maintainer becomes the bottleneck. Not because contributors write badly, but because there is no automated layer between "a draft was submitted" and "it is ready to merge."

Invisible Mentors fills that gap.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
Every pull request runs through a two-layer automated pipeline. No human reviewer is needed until the writing is already clean.

flowchart TD
    A([Contributor Opens PR]) --> B{Vale Scan\nJargon Ruleset}
    B -->|Writing is clean| C([Auto-Deploy\nto GitHub Pages])
    B -->|Jargon detected| D([Gemini 2.5 Flash\nAI Analysis])
    D --> E([Structured Feedback\nPosted as PR Comment])
    E --> F([Contributor\nRevises and Pushes])
    F --> B

    style A fill:#312e81,stroke:#6366f1,color:#e0e7ff
    style B fill:#1e3a5f,stroke:#3b82f6,color:#dbeafe
    style C fill:#064e3b,stroke:#10b981,color:#d1fae5
    style D fill:#4c1d95,stroke:#8b5cf6,color:#ede9fe
    style E fill:#4c1d95,stroke:#8b5cf6,color:#ede9fe
    style F fill:#1e3a5f,stroke:#3b82f6,color:#dbeafe

What this means in practice

A contributor gets detailed writing feedback in under 30 seconds, well before any maintainer has had a chance to open the PR.

docs.title: Core Features
docs.text:
• Runs on Every PR Automatically

    Triggered by GitHub Actions on each pull request. No per-contributor setup, no accounts, no friction.

  • Gemini 2.5 Flash

    When jargon is detected, Gemini reads the flagged text and generates a structured rewrite with alternatives and context, not just a flag.

  • Vale Prose Linting

    Custom ruleset catches corporate buzzwords before they land in your docs: leverage, utilize, paradigm, synergy.

  • Native GitHub Integration

    Feedback posts as a sticky PR comment. Contributors stay in GitHub. No webhooks, no external dashboards to manage.

  • 30-Second Feedback Loop

    Scan, analyze, comment. The full pipeline runs in under 30 seconds from push to feedback.

  • MIT Licensed

    Fork it, adapt it, make it your own. Built to be shared with anyone who wants it.

docs.title: Request / Response Flow
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    actor C as Contributor
    participant GH as GitHub
    participant GHA as GitHub Actions
    participant Vale as Vale Linter
    participant AI as Gemini 2.5 Flash
    participant Pages as GitHub Pages

    C->>GH: git push (opens PR)
    GH->>GHA: Trigger: on pull_request
    GHA->>Vale: vale --config .vale.ini docs/
    alt Writing is clean
        Vale-->>GHA: exit 0
        GHA->>Pages: mkdocs gh-deploy
        Pages-->>C: Docs live at GitHub Pages
    else Jargon detected
        Vale-->>GHA: exit 1 with findings
        GHA->>AI: Send flagged text
        AI-->>GHA: Structured rewrite table
        GHA->>GH: Post sticky PR comment
        GH-->>C: Notification with feedback
    end

28s Average time to first feedback

2-Layer Automated review pipeline

0 Human reviewers needed

MIT Open source license

docs.title: Try It Yourself
docs.text:
Fork and test in 3 steps

1. Fork the repository 

# Click "Fork" on GitHub, then clone your fork
git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/Invisible-Mentors.git
cd Invisible-Mentors

2. Add jargon to a doc 

echo "\n\nWe need to leverage our existing paradigms to synergize outcomes." \
  >> docs/onboarding.md
git add docs/onboarding.md
git commit -m "test: add jargon for demo"
git push origin main

3. Open a Pull Request

Watch the pipeline run, Vale flag the jargon, and Gemini post a structured rewrite directly to your PR, all within 30 seconds.

Fork on GitHub

Presented at Linux Foundation Open Source Summit, May 2026

This project was built to address a real issue that comes up in almost every growing open source project: maintainers spending too much time on documentation review. Automating that feedback loop lets maintainers focus on work only they can do, and gives contributors a faster path to getting their changes merged.

Built by Sai Sravan Cherukuri**

docs.title: Live Demo Snippet
docs.text: we should leverage our paradigms

docs.title: Contributor Guide
docs.text:
Welcome to Invisible Mentors. This guide explains how to contribute documentation improvements, how the automated pipeline reviews your work, and what to do when you receive feedback.

Quick Summary

Every pull request is reviewed automatically. If your writing is clear and jargon-free, your docs deploy instantly. If jargon is detected, Gemini AI posts structured feedback directly to your PR — no waiting, no guessing.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• A GitHub account
  • git installed locally
  • Basic familiarity with Markdown
  • (Optional) Python 3.10+ and mkdocs-material for local preview

docs.title: Fork and Clone
docs.text:
# 1. Fork the repo via GitHub, then clone your fork
git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/Invisible-Mentors.git
cd Invisible-Mentors

# 2. Create a feature branch
git checkout -b docs/improve-onboarding

# 3. (Optional) Set up local preview
pip install mkdocs-material
mkdocs serve
# → Preview at http://localhost:8000

docs.title: Understanding the Pipeline
docs.text:
When you open a pull request, this is exactly what happens:

flowchart LR
    PR([Your PR]) --> V{Vale\nScan}
    V -->|Clean ✅| D([Deploy to\nGitHub Pages])
    V -->|Jargon ⚠️| G([Gemini AI\nAnalysis])
    G --> C([PR Comment\nwith Rewrite])
    C --> R([You Revise\n& Push])
    R --> V

    style PR fill:#312e81,stroke:#6366f1,color:#e0e7ff
    style V fill:#1e3a5f,stroke:#3b82f6,color:#dbeafe
    style D fill:#064e3b,stroke:#10b981,color:#d1fae5
    style G fill:#4c1d95,stroke:#8b5cf6,color:#ede9fe
    style C fill:#4c1d95,stroke:#8b5cf6,color:#ede9fe
    style R fill:#1e3a5f,stroke:#3b82f6,color:#dbeafe

What Vale checks for

Vale scans all .md files in docs/ against a custom jargon ruleset. The following words and phrases are flagged:

Jargon Better Alternative leverage use utilize use paradigm approach, model, pattern synergy / synergize collaboration, working together leverage existing infrastructure use what's already there innovative solution solution (drop the filler)

docs.title: Step 1 — Edit a Document
docs.text:
All contributor-facing documentation lives in docs/. Make your edits in Markdown:

# Example: improve the onboarding guide
nano docs/onboarding.md

# Or edit any file in docs/
ls docs/

docs.title: Step 2 — Preview Locally (Optional)
docs.text:
mkdocs serve

Open http://localhost:8000 to see your changes rendered with the full Material theme, including Mermaid diagrams.

docs.title: Step 3 — Commit and Push
docs.text:
git add docs/
git commit -m "docs: improve contributor onboarding guide"
git push origin docs/improve-onboarding

docs.title: Step 4 — Open a Pull Request
docs.text: Open your pull request on GitHub. Within seconds, the Invisible Mentor pipeline triggers automatically.

docs.title: Reading AI Feedback
docs.text:
If Vale detects jargon, Gemini AI will post a comment like this to your PR:

Sample AI Feedback Comment

## 🤖 Invisible Mentor — Jargon Audit

| # | Location | Flagged Phrase | Suggested Rewrite | Reason |
|---|----------|---------------|-------------------|--------|
| 1 | onboarding.md:14 | "leverage existing paradigms" | "use existing approaches" | Avoid corporate buzzwords |
| 2 | onboarding.md:22 | "synergize outcomes" | "improve results together" | Overly abstract |

**Revise the flagged passages and push a new commit to re-run the check.**

Read the table, update the flagged lines, and push again. The pipeline re-runs automatically.

docs.title: Writing Style Guide
docs.text:
Follow these principles to pass the Vale check on your first try:

Do Don't
  • Write in plain English — say what you mean directly
  • Use active voice: "The pipeline checks your PR" not "Your PR is checked by the pipeline"
  • Use specific, concrete words instead of vague qualifiers
  • Keep sentences under 25 words when possible
  • Define technical terms the first time you use them
  • Use corporate buzzwords: leverage, utilize, synergize, paradigm
  • Start sentences with "In order to" (write "To" instead)
  • Use passive voice as a default
  • Pad sentences with filler: "innovative", "cutting-edge", "world-class"
  • Write walls of text — break things up with lists and headers

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
Invisible-Mentors/
├── docs/                    # ← All documentation (what Vale checks)
│   ├── index.md             # Home page
│   ├── onboarding.md        # This guide
│   ├── assets/              # Images, logo
│   └── stylesheets/         # Custom CSS
├── vale-styles/             # Custom Vale jargon rules
│   └── Jargon/
│       └── jargon.yml
├── .vale.ini                # Vale configuration
├── mkdocs.yml               # Docs site configuration
├── ai_mentor.py             # Gemini AI integration script
├── mentor_persona.txt       # AI persona definition
└── .github/
    └── workflows/
        ├── main.yml         # Docs lint + deploy (on push to main)
        └── invisible-mentor.yml  # PR review pipeline

docs.title: Demo: The Pipeline in Action
docs.text:
The docs/onboarding.md file intentionally contains jargon below — this is what gets flagged during a live conference demo.

Live Demo Zone

The following section contains jargon that Vale will catch. This is intentional — it demonstrates the pipeline's detection capability live at conferences.

docs.title: Legacy Onboarding Content (Demo Zone)
docs.text:
We need to utilize our existing toolchain to leverage the paradigms established in the current architecture. By synergizing our documentation efforts with the development workflow, we can ensure that all stakeholders are aligned with the strategic objectives.

The implementation utilizes a multi-layered approach to paradigm enforcement, ensuring that contributors can leverage the full potential of the Vale ruleset while synergizing their contributions with the existing codebase.

Need Help?

Open a GitHub Discussion or check the project README for more context.
